task_goal: land safely on the landing pad using observable state evidence, without using the official reward

observation_schema.name: x_position
observation_schema.meaning: horizontal coordinate of the lander; landing pad at x=0

observation_schema.name: y_position
observation_schema.meaning: vertical coordinate of the lander; landing pad at y=0

observation_schema.name: x_velocity
observation_schema.meaning: horizontal linear velocity of the lander

observation_schema.name: y_velocity
observation_schema.meaning: vertical linear velocity of the lander

observation_schema.name: angle
observation_schema.meaning: rotation angle of the lander in radians

observation_schema.name: angular_velocity
observation_schema.meaning: angular velocity of the lander in radians per second (scaled by 0.4 internally, but observation is in 0.4 rad/s units per source note)

observation_schema.name: leg1_contact
observation_schema.meaning: boolean indicating whether the first leg is in contact with the ground

observation_schema.name: leg2_contact
observation_schema.meaning: boolean indicating whether the second leg is in contact with the ground

termination_signals: lander crashes (body contacts the moon surface)

lander goes outside viewport (x coordinate > 1 or < -1? Actually |x| > 1 triggers termination but not specified precisely in the summary; the summary says 'x coordinate is greater than 1', so likely symmetric bounds)
lander is not awake (body comes to rest and does not move/collide, from Box2D sleep state)
labeling_cautions: Do not use or infer the official environment reward.
Do not treat long episode length alone as success_like.
Do not treat high approach progress with unstable final speed or large angle as success_like.
Do not treat a crash outside the pad as success_like even if legs touch ground.

Both legs must be in contact with the ground and the lander must be near the pad (x near 0) for a safe landing; unstable final state (high speed, large angle) should not be classified as success_like.
The angular velocity observation is scaled by 0.4 internally; raw values should be interpreted with caution.
unknowns: Exact boundary values for viewport termination (the summary says x > 1, but low bound for x is -1.5 in observation space, so likely symmetric, but not explicitly stated).
The precise condition for 'lander not awake' termination and how it manifests in observations.
